title: BlackRock Expands Onchain Funds as Tokenization Surges
summary: BlackRock is filing to offer new tokenized funds, joining a 200% year-over-year growth in real-world assets on the blockchain. This move could make investing in traditional assets more accessible.
body:
BlackRock, the world's largest asset manager, has filed paperwork to expand its lineup of tokenized funds. Tokenization is the process of converting real-world assets like stocks, bonds, or real estate into digital tokens on a blockchain. This move comes as the market for tokenized real-world assets has grown 200% over the past year, according to industry reports.

This development matters because it could make investing in traditional assets more accessible and efficient. Tokenized funds allow investors to buy and sell fractions of assets with lower fees and faster settlement times. Think of it like buying a piece of a fund that's backed by real-world assets, but with the speed and transparency of blockchain technology.

If you're interested in this trend, keep an eye out for BlackRock's new tokenized fund offerings. While these products may initially be aimed at institutional investors, they could eventually become available to retail investors. This could be a game-changer for anyone looking to diversify their portfolio with traditional assets in a more modern way.
twitterThread:
🚀 Big news: BlackRock is expanding its tokenized funds. Here's what it means for you.

Tokenization is turning real-world assets into digital tokens. Think stocks, bonds, or real estate — but on the blockchain.

This isn't just for crypto nerds. It's about making investing easier and more accessible.

BlackRock, the world's largest asset manager, just filed paperwork to expand its tokenized fund lineup.

Why? Because the market for tokenized real-world assets has grown 200% over the past year.

In plain English: Tokenized funds let you buy and sell fractions of assets with lower fees and faster settlement times.

Think of it like buying a piece of a fund that's backed by real-world assets, but with the speed and transparency of blockchain.

The part that matters: This could make investing in traditional assets more accessible and efficient.

If you've ever wanted to invest in stocks or bonds but found the process cumbersome, this could be a game-changer.

Here's what you can do: Keep an eye out for BlackRock's new tokenized fund offerings. While these products may initially be aimed at institutional investors, they could eventually become available to retail investors.
